title: Conjecture 1 (Kreutzer, Oum, Seymour, van der Zypen and Wood)
context_text: Kreutzer, Oum, Seymour, van der Zypen and Wood proved every digraph is majority 4-colorable and conjectured the bound can be reduced to 3. Odd directed cycles show at least 3 colors are sometimes necessary, but no digraph requiring 4 colors is known. The paper's main motivation is to verify this conjecture for sparse digraphs, complementing existing probabilistic results for dense digraphs.
attributed_to: Kreutzer, Oum, Seymour, van der Zypen and Wood
paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture for digraphs with chromatic number at most 6 or dichromatic number at most 3; proves the stronger majority 3-choosability for digraphs with $\Delta^+(D) \leq 4$ or $\Delta(D) \leq 7$, which covers 3-regular and 4-regular digraphs.

title: Fractional Majority Coloring Weight Question (Kreutzer et al.)
statement_text: What is the smallest constant $K$ such that every digraph admits a fractional majority coloring with total weight at most $K$?
context_text: Kreutzer et al. introduced fractional majority coloring as an LP-relaxation of majority coloring and raised the question of determining the optimal constant $K$. The trivial bound $K \leq 4$ follows from majority 4-colorability; proving $K \leq 3$ would be an easier analogue of the majority 3-coloring conjecture.
attributed_to: Kreutzer, Oum, Seymour, van der Zypen and Wood
paper_contribution: Shows $K \leq 3.9602$ (Theorem 6) via a probabilistic coloring with deterministic alteration; also shows that every digraph $D$ with $\delta^+(D) \geq C(1/\varepsilon)^2 \ln(2/\varepsilon)$ has a fractional majority $(2+\varepsilon)$-coloring (Theorem 7).

title: Majority 2-Colorability Recognition Question (Kreutzer et al.)
statement_text: Is there a characterisation of digraphs that have a majority 2-coloring, or a polynomial time algorithm to recognise such digraphs?
context_text: Kreutzer et al. posed the question of whether majority 2-colorable digraphs admit an efficient recognition algorithm or a structural characterisation. The paper answers this negatively, resolving the open problem.
attributed_to: Kreutzer, Oum, Seymour, van der Zypen and Wood
paper_contribution: Answers the question negatively by proving that deciding whether a given digraph is majority 2-colorable is NP-complete (Theorem 9).
notes: Section 6 of the paper, which reportedly contains additional open problems posed by the authors, was not included in the provided text and could not be extracted.
